name: Yup
what_it_does: JavaScript object schema validation library with native async support, chainable builder API, and first-class integration with form libraries like Formik.
use_cases: Validate form submission payloads asynchronously with .validate() returning a Promise, including server-side uniqueness checks
Collect all field validation errors at once using { abortEarly: false } to return a complete error map to a UI or agent
Use built-in string/number/date/boolean validators (email, url, min, max, matches) to validate common field types without custom code
Define conditional schema shape with .when() to apply different validation rules based on other field values at runtime
Integrate with Formik's validationSchema prop to provide form-level validation in React applications an agent is scaffolding
not_for: TypeScript-first projects where static type inference from schema is required — Zod's z.infer<> is more ergonomic for that
Non-form data validation at high throughput — Yup's async-by-default design adds overhead when synchronous parsing suffices
Environments where bundle size is tightly constrained — Yup is heavier than Valibot and not tree-shakeable
best_when: Validating user-submitted forms in JavaScript applications where async validators (remote checks) and rich error messages for all fields are required.
avoid_when: You need synchronous parse-and-type behavior or compile-time TypeScript type inference from schema definitions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError includes a structured errors array and inner array of sub-errors when abortEarly:false — machine-readable but less structured than ZodError.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure validation function with no side effects — repeated calls on the same input return the same result.
known_agent_gotchas: abortEarly defaults to true — agents expecting all errors will miss subsequent failures unless explicitly setting { abortEarly: false }
.validate() returns a Promise even for synchronous schemas — agents must always await or chain .then() to get the result
Yup casts values by default (e.g., '5' becomes 5 for number fields) — agents relying on strict type checking must use .strict() mode
.when() conditional validators are evaluated at parse time and require careful ordering — a referenced field must be declared before the .when() call
TypeScript inference from Yup schemas is incomplete without yup.InferType<typeof schema> and still less precise than Zod's inference

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure client-side library with no network access; security depends on how the calling application handles validation errors and whether untrusted schema definitions are accepted.
